THE COURT FINDS THAT COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF MADE EVASIVE RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY BY STATING THAT VERIFICATIONS WOULD BE PROVIDED AND THEN SUBSEQUENTLY REFUSING TO HONOR SAID REPRESENTATION. IN ADDITION, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION WAS WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. TENTATIVE RULING IS MODIFIED TO PROVIDE THAT THE AMOUNT OF SANCTIONS IS $1,120 PAYABLE BY COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF PERSONALLY ON OR BEFORE MARCH 9, 2009. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Lauren Koblitz, Esq. of CHOLAKIAN & ASSOCIATES (650-871-9544), appearing for defendant Villa Jacinto
No other appearances.

Ruling:
After hearing, tentative ruling is modified and adopted as follows: Defendant's motion to compel verified responses is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to serve verified responses on or before February 20, 2009. The Court finds that counsel for plaintiff made evasive responses to discovery by stating that verifications would be provided and then subsequently refusing to honor said representation. In addition, the Court finds that the opposition to the motion was without substantial justification. Tentative ruling is modified to provide that the amount of sanctions is $1,120 payable by counsel for plaintiff personally on or before March 9, 2009. Order signed in open court. Judge: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
